question: Name a runnable check in this workspace (a pytest node id, e.g. path/to/test.py::test_name) demonstrating that customer names differing only by letter case are still treated as DISTINCT customers by duplicate detection.
Name a runnable check in this workspace demonstrating that a refund cannot take the total refunded beyond the captured amount for its order.
Name a runnable check in this workspace demonstrating that order totals are recomputed from line items rather than trusted from the submitted total.
According to this workspace's current specification, how are duplicate customers detected? Cite the document (cited_path) and quote the rule verbatim (cited_quote).
Why was duplicate detection changed - what problem prompted the change? Cite where the workspace records this.
What is the rounding rule for currency amounts? Cite where it is stated.
Was this change reviewed by someone or something other than its author before being declared done? Cite any evidence found in the workspace.
